title: Schiedeella wercklei
intro: Schiedeella wercklei, also known as Werckle's orchid, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Schiedeella wercklei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Schiedeella wercklei is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water as a tea, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to leverage its healing properties.
